Question: Considering measurements from these four specific observations — (1) FluxBeaconID "FB-LaSelva-std: lowland tropical wet forest flux station" in the "riparian gallery forest ribbon along ephemeral stream corridors" biome metacluster with canopy litter "moderate litter mantle with distinct fermentation microstrata" and hydrological connectivity "isolated hillslope patch with negligible overland flow connectivity"; (2) FluxBeaconID "FB-KonzaPrairie-LTER: tallgrass prairie turbulent flux node" in the "subalpine conifer krummholz patch-network over discontinuous permafrost" biome metacluster with canopy litter "incipient mull layer beneath high-litter deciduous canopy" and hydrological connectivity "isolated hillslope patch with negligible overland flow connectivity"; (3) FluxBeaconID "FB-Tapajos-Flux: tropical rainforest eddy footprint array" in the "temperate mixedwood alluvial corridor with successional mosaics" biome metacluster with canopy litter "stratified litter-fermentation complex above compacted peat" and hydrological connectivity "moderate micro-topographic connectivity supporting seasonal seepage"; and (4) FluxBeaconID "FB-LaSelva-std: lowland tropical wet forest flux station" in the "old-field secondary succession matrix with heterogenous litter accumulation" biome metacluster with canopy litter "continuous mor-like profile with dense undecomposed mat" and hydrological connectivity "artificially fragmented drainage mosaic with engineered conveyance" — what is the average soil respiration rate across these four observations?
